summary: A brief historical overview from the Library of Congress detailing Henry Ford's 1913 implementation of the moving assembly line. The text highlights how cross-industry inspiration led to massive efficiency gains, allowing Ford to drastically reduce the price of the Model T and make automobiles accessible to the middle class.

principles.statement: Use manufacturing efficiency to lower costs and pass the savings directly to the consumer.
principles.rationale: By drastically reducing the time it takes to build a product, production costs fall. Passing these savings on makes the product affordable to the middle class, transforming it from a luxury into a mass-market necessity.

mental_models.name: Cross-Industry Inspiration
mental_models.description: Looking outside of one's own field to observe how other industries solve similar operational problems, then adapting those processes to your own context.
mental_models.example: Ford observed the continuous flow and production processes used in bakeries, mills, breweries, and meat packing plants, and adapted them to design the moving assembly line for automobile manufacturing.
